D002.final_answer: q1: no, sprinklers do not run. q2: yes, refill valve activates. q3: pump activates the refill valve and sprinklers still do not run (tank is at 15% which is below 20%, so the sprinkler path is not taken)
working: Rain sensor reports NO rain at 6 AM -> timer activates pump.
Tank level 15% is <= 20% -> pump activates refill valve, not sprinklers.
Q1 answer: sprinklers do NOT run this cycle.
Q2 answer: refill valve activates to refill tank from mains.
Q3: without rain sensor the timer unconditionally activates pump. Tank still 15% (<=20%), so refill valve activates and sprinklers still do not run.

D003.final_answer: q1: n4 activates at t=2. q2: n5 first activates at t=1. q3: n3, n4, n5 never activate. q4: n4 activates at t=1
working: T=0: N1 -> N2 and N3.
N2 has 2-step delay: N4 receives from N2 at T=2.
N3 has 1-step delay: N4 receives from N3 at T=1; N5 activates immediately at T=1 from N3's message.
Q1: N4 needs both; second arrival T=2 -> N4 activates at T=2.
Q2: N5 first activates at T=1.

Q3: Remove N1->N3. N3 never messaged -> N3 never activates. N4 never gets N3's msg -> N4 never activates. N5 never receives (not from N3, and N4 never activates to send). N3, N4, N5 all never activate.
Q4: N2 delay=0 -> N4 receives from N2 at T=0 and from N3 at T=1; second arrival T=1 -> N4 activates at T=1.
Pattern: @ followed by k hashes, k incrementing 1, 2, 3, 4, ...
Previous complete group had 4 hashes.
Next complete group: @ followed by 5 hashes.

E002.final_answer: m l k, p o n, s r q
working: Each example reverses every row horizontally (columns flipped left-to-right).
Apply to K L M / N O P / Q R S -> M L K / P O N / S R Q.
T1 reverses the string: PARK -> KRAP.
T2 shifts each letter +1 with alphabet wrap: K->L, R->S, A->B, P->Q.
Final: LSBQ.
